title: Ipomoea macrantha
intro: Ipomoea macrantha, also known as the large-flowered morning glory, is a species of flowering plant in the Convolvulaceae family. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory issues, due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Ipomoea macrantha include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which have been reported to exhibit antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and immunomodulatory activities. In traditional herbal medicine, Ipomoea macrantha is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are consumed to alleviate symptoms of colds, coughs, and fever.
taxonomy: Ipomoea macrantha is a species of flowering plant in the morning glory family, Convolvulaceae. It belongs to the genus Ipomoea, which consists of around 600-700 species. The Convolvulaceae family is a large and diverse group of plants with over 1,800 species worldwide. The genus Ipomoea is further divided into several sections based on morphological characteristics. Ipomoea macrantha is classified as a member of the section Calonyction.
